meta.title: Mott Larbe — learn Chechen through reading
meta.description: Language platform: real texts with click-to-translate, morphological analysis and personal vocabulary. Levels A1–C2.

hero.sub: Real texts with instant click-to-translate, morphological breakdown and a personal vocabulary. No tab-switching, no dictionary lookups.
hero.readerOriginal: Цхьа де тхо хьалха гIайгIане хилларехь, амма цул тIаьхьа кхета луучу хан йоьссира.
hero.readerTranslation: At dawn we walked quietly, and then I began to understand.

features.title: Everything you need to read in Chechen
features.sub: The platform is built around one idea: every word is an entry point into the dictionary. Here's what that gives you in practice.

click.title: Click → translation
click.desc: Click any word — a card appears instantly: translation, base form, grammatical form, examples.

morphology.title: Morphology
morphology.desc: The complex grammar of Chechen is parsed automatically: cases, classes, tenses. Lemmatization and normalization.

dictionary.title: Personal vocabulary
dictionary.desc: Saved words, folders, statuses (new / learning / known), statistics and quick search through your entries.

srs.title: Spaced repetition
srs.desc: Proprietary Esalar system (3 000+ words learned): NEW → OLD → RETIRED → numbered Esalar. Plus classic SM-2 algorithm. Load is gradual — cards accumulate on their own.

texts.title: Real texts
texts.desc: Literature, history, folktales, news. Texts with A1–C2 levels, tags, reading time and automatic difficulty scoring.

progress.title: Progress and statistics
progress.desc: Streaks, weekly activity, text progress, word-by-level breakdown. See what you learned and when.

how.title: Three steps to fluent reading
how.sub: No need to cram word lists — learn the language in its natural environment.

choose.title: Pick a text
choose.desc: Library by levels A1–C2 with tags, difficulty score and reading time. Want it simpler — pick A1.

read.title: Read and click
read.desc: Unfamiliar word — click. Translation, base form and grammar appear right in the text. One click — added to vocabulary.

review.title: Reinforce by review
review.desc: A short review session every day. The system knows which words to show today and which to postpone.

demo.title: Reading as it is
demo.sub: This is what a regular reading session looks like. Highlights show what the system knows about each word.
demo.textTitle: Chechen folklore · A young man's tale

levels.title: For every level — A1 to C2
levels.sub: The platform adapts: start with the simplest texts or go straight to advanced reading.

testimonials.title: What our users say
testimonials.sub: Real stories from people learning Chechen.

items.text: I can finally read Chechen texts and understand them. Word-by-word translation is exactly what I was missing.
items.name: Maryam I.

items.text: Morphological analysis helped me grasp the grammar. I noticed real progress within a month.
items.name: Islam Kh.

items.text: I wanted to reclaim my ancestral language — Mott Larbe gave me the right tool. I read every day now.
items.name: Ruslan D.

languages.title: Supported languages
languages.sub: Two languages available now. New directions added on request.

items.name: Chechen
items.desc: Vainakh language of the Nakh-Dagestanian family. About 1.5 million speakers. Cyrillic script since 1938.

items.name: Russian
items.desc: East Slavic language of the Indo-European family. Official language of the Russian Federation.

pricing.title: Simple pricing, no surprises
pricing.sub: Start free. When you want more — switch to Premium or Pro.

items.q: What level can I start at?
items.a: Any. The platform suits both complete beginners (A1) and those who already read fluently (C1–C2). Texts are organised by level, so you can begin with the simplest.

items.q: How does «Esalar» differ from regular spaced repetition?
items.a: They're two independent systems. SuperMemo 2 is the classic algorithm with intervals and 0–5 ratings. Esalar is a proprietary system: words are distributed across decks (NEW → OLD → NUMBERED), numbered Esalar rotate every day. Use either — or both.
Yes, anytime in one click. Access to paid features stays until the end of the paid period — then the account switches to the free plan automatically without losing data.

items.q: What happens to my vocabulary if I stop using the service?
items.a: Your personal vocabulary, text progress and review history are preserved as long as the account exists. They can be exported anytime.
Currently — Chechen (CHE) with translation to Russian (RU). The architecture supports adding other languages; new directions will be added based on community requests.
The web version is adapted for phones and tablets — opens in a browser and works like a native app. Dedicated iOS/Android apps are planned.

cta.sub: Free plan — no credit card. A couple of clicks until your first text.
cta.foot: You can switch to a paid plan anytime. Cancel in one click.

footer.about: A language platform for learning Chechen through reading real texts with instant translation.

nav.freePlanDesc: Up to 200 words. Unlock more features.

meta.title: Review — Mott Larbe
meta.description: Spaced repetition for Chechen words: SuperMemo 2 algorithm and the custom Esalar system.

intro.title: Time to review
intro.subtitle: {count} words are due for review. SuperMemo 2 will compute the optimal interval for each.
intro.empty: Nothing to review today. Come back tomorrow.
intro.error: Failed to load words. Please try again later.

stats.knownHint: Words with a review interval ≥ 21 days

done.title: SuperMemo 2 session complete!
done.subtitle: All words reviewed. Next dates have been scheduled.

guide.title: How SuperMemo 2 Works
guide.intro: SuperMemo 2 is a spaced repetition algorithm developed in 1987. It shows each word exactly when you are about to forget it — forcing your brain to work a little harder and reliably moving the word into long-term memory. The better you know a word, the longer the system waits before showing it again.
guide.why: Human memory works like this: if you review a word just before it fades, the next interval roughly doubles. SuperMemo 2 exploits this "spacing effect" automatically. 200 words take about the same time as 20 — because well-learned words almost never appear.

stages.name: New
stages.desc: Word just added to the vocabulary. No history yet — SuperMemo 2 will see it for the first time.

stages.name: Learning
stages.desc: Word is being drilled. It appears frequently — first after a few minutes, then after a day. Once the interval grows long enough, it graduates to Review.

stages.name: Review
stages.desc: Word is learned. The interval grows with each correct answer: 3 days → 7 days → 15 days → 1 month and so on.

stages.name: Relearning
stages.desc: You pressed "Forgot" on a Review-stage word. The algorithm resets the interval and runs the word through short repetitions before returning it to Review.

rating.label: Юха а
rating.desc: You couldn't recall the word at all. Interval resets; word reappears later in this session.

rating.label: Hard
rating.desc: Recalled with effort. Interval shrinks slightly; word returns soon.

rating.label: Good
rating.desc: Recalled normally. Interval grows by the standard SuperMemo 2 formula.

rating.label: Easy
rating.desc: Word felt trivial. A longer-than-usual interval is set — it will appear less often.

tips: Rate honestly. Pressing "Easy" on everything means words appear rarely and you start forgetting them.
A short session every day beats a long one once a week: SuperMemo 2 is designed for a steady rhythm.
Don't try to memorise a word on the first pass — the Learning stage is exactly for that.
If sessions feel too long, check how many words are due in your SuperMemo 2 settings.
A word in Review with a 30-day interval is a word you genuinely know. Don't worry about the gap.

intro.title: Esalar — Mott Larbe
intro.subtitle:
Esalar (from Chechen «эсан лар» — a trace in memory) is a personal vocabulary system — built and proven in practice.
I started with 5 words a day on paper flashcards. No apps, no algorithms — just a stack of cards and 10 minutes every morning. A few months later I was reviewing over 600 words a day and it felt effortless. That's how accumulation works: words are added slowly, the brain keeps up — and one day you catch yourself actually knowing the language.

row.oldDesc: Pushed out of NEW when it overflowed

premium.title: Esalar — Premium feature
premium.subtitle: The custom learning system is available with a subscription. This is a demo interface.

settings.title: Esalar settings
settings.deckMaxSizeHint: Maximum cards in each deck (NEW, OLD, RETIRED)

daily.title: Words for today
daily.subtitle: Add them to Esalar to start learning
daily.empty: No new words — all dictionary words are already in Esalar
daily.emptyHint: Read texts to grow your vocabulary

done.title: Esalar complete!
done.subtitle: The next numbered deck unlocks tomorrow.

guide.title: How Esalar works
guide.intro: Esalar (from Chechen «эсан лар» — a trace in memory) is a proprietary structured vocabulary method. It was originally developed for learning English using physical flashcards: the author started with 5 words a day, gradually moved up to 10, kept 90 cards per stack — and eventually reviewed 600+ words daily without feeling any strain. The same system, now digital, has been used to learn over 3 000 words in Chechen.
guide.why: The secret is that the workload grows imperceptibly. On day one you add 5 words and review only those. The next day you add 5 more and review 10. Words accumulate gradually: after a month 100–150 are in circulation, after three months — 300–500. With a deck of 90 cards and a pace of 10 words a day, you quietly reach 600+ daily reviews — yet it never feels heavy, because your brain already recognizes most words automatically. We recommend starting with 3–5 words and raising the bar only when the session starts to feel easy.

how.name: Керла
how.desc: All new words land here. This deck is reviewed every day — the word is fresh and needs reinforcement. Once the deck fills up, the oldest word moves to OLD.

how.name: Хьалхалера
how.desc: Words that graduated from NEW. Reviewed daily alongside NEW. When OLD fills up, the oldest word moves to RETIRED.

how.name: Тохарлера
how.desc: Archive deck. Words are reviewed daily along with the others. When RETIRED fills up, words move into numbered decks.

how.name: Numbered Esalar
how.desc: Words are spread across numbered stacks (1, 2, 3…). One or more rotate in each day. This is long-term storage: a word comes back every few days or weeks.

how.name: Юха
how.desc: If you tap 'Move to REPEAT' during a session, the word lands here and is reviewed every day until you tap 'Return to deck'. Use it for words that just won't stick.

tips: Start with 3–5 words a day. Don't rush to increase — let the system build momentum.
Don't skip days. Missed cards don't disappear — they pile up and land on the next session.
If a word won't stick, send it to REPEAT. Daily contact fixes it quickly.
10 words a day = ~300 per month = ~3 600 per year.
After 2–3 months the session becomes a habit: your brain will recognize most words automatically.

rating.label: Дагахь хаа
rating.desc: You recognized the word — it moves forward along the path.

rating.label: Юха а
rating.desc: You didn't remember — the word stays in place and reappears later in the same session.

settings.label: New words per day
settings.desc: How many words are suggested for adding daily. We recommend 3–5 to start, 10 for active build-up.

settings.label: Numbered decks per day
settings.desc: How many numbered decks activate per session (1–5). Increase when the session feels too short.

settings.label: Esalar size
settings.desc: Maximum cards in each of the NEW / OLD / RETIRED stacks. When the limit is hit, the word automatically advances to the next level.

settings.title: Gemini API
settings.description: The key is used for AI translation of words not found in the dictionary. It is stored encrypted on the server (AES-256).
settings.keyHint: Your key is stored encrypted on the server
settings.freeNote: Gemini Flash Lite is free — no payment required. Just a Google account.
settings.step1Post: and sign in with your Google account
settings.step2: Click "Create API key" and copy the generated key
settings.step3: Paste the key into the field below and click "Save key"
settings.securityNote: Your key is encrypted with AES-256 before being stored on the server. Neither the team nor third parties can read it.
settings.languageDescription: Choose the language words and phrases will be translated into.
settings.modelDescription: Choose a model. It determines speed, accuracy, and billing.
settings.billingNote: Translation costs are billed directly to your Google AI Studio account. The developer takes no commission.
settings.disclaimerItem1: Gemini is an artificial intelligence product by Google. Mott Larbe is not the owner, developer, or partner of Gemini. We only provide the ability to connect your personal API key to our platform.
settings.disclaimerItem2: The quality and accuracy of translations depend entirely on the Gemini model. Mott Larbe is not responsible for inaccurate or incorrect translations generated by AI.
settings.disclaimerItem3: A Mott Larbe Premium subscription gives access to platform features — lessons, dictionary, and reader. It does not include or cover Gemini usage costs. Gemini Flash and Pro models with higher limits require a separate paid Google AI Studio key.
settings.disclaimerItem4: For residents of the Chechen Republic and Russia: Gemini is not available in these regions without a VPN. A VPN with a server outside Russia is required for AI translation to work. Mott Larbe does not provide VPN services.
settings.disclaimerItem5: Translations are cached on Mott Larbe servers in anonymised form — without any link to your account. This is done to speed up repeated requests and reduce load on your key. Cached data is used solely within the platform.
settings.disclaimerItem6: Your Gemini API key is stored encrypted (AES-256) and is accessible to no one but you. Neither the platform owner nor Mott Larbe staff can technically read or use it. The key is used solely to send your requests to Gemini.

popup.recorded: We recorded this word and will try to add it to the dictionary
popup.noKeyDescription: Add a Gemini API key in your profile to enable AI translation of words not found in the dictionary.

popup.addKeyPrompt: Add a Gemini API key in settings to get AI translations

popup.errorLocationNotSupported: Gemini is not available in your region. Try using a VPN.
popup.languageDescription: Choose the language words and phrases will be translated into while reading.

phrase.refinePlaceholder: E.g.: «ka» here means a ram in the literal sense...

models.modelFlashLiteDescription: Best for everyday reading. Lightning-fast, virtually impossible to exhaust the free quota. Handles 90% of basic words and short phrases.
models.modelFlashDescription: The balanced choice. Understands idioms, modern prose and hidden context far better than Lite, without making you wait. Needs a paid key at high volume.
models.modelProDescription: Maximum accuracy for classic literature and poetry. Handles archaisms, rare metaphors and complex structures. Slower — recommended only with a paid key.
models.modelFlashLiteFreeNote: Free: up to 15 req/min, 1,000/day · Paid: up to 300/min, 1,500/day
models.modelProFreeNote: Free: up to 15 req/min, 100/day · Paid: up to 150/min, 250/day
models.modelFlashFreeNote: Free: up to 10 req/min, 250/day · Paid: up to 300/min, 1,500/day

fallback.switched: Your model may have hit its per-minute limit — switched to Flash Lite for now. We'll retry your model in about a minute.
fallback.persistentBanner: It looks like your model's limits are exhausted for today, or no billing card is attached. Translations are continuing via Flash Lite — keep reading without interruption. Your model will return automatically when limits reset.
